question: If there's no upfront cost, what's the catch?
answer: None. The designs have to win you over before anything else happens. See them, and if they're not for you, walk away and owe nothing. Only once you're happy do I build it, with your first payment held back until the site is live, so there's nothing to pay upfront and nothing to risk in finding out. No deposit, no build fee, just designs that have to earn your yes.

question: Am I tied into a contract, and what happens if I cancel?
answer: There's a six-month minimum to start, which gives your new site the time it needs to prove itself. After that, you're on a simple month-to-month basis. If you ever choose to move on, your domain, content, and copy are always yours, but you don't keep the site.

question: Why hand-coded instead of Wix, Squarespace, or WordPress?
answer: Template builders are quick to start but slow to load, and a slow site quietly costs you diners before they ever see the menu. Hand-coded means a site that loads fast, ranks higher on Google, and is shaped around your venue to turn visitors into bookings, not squeezed into someone else's template.

question: Does a faster site really bring in more bookings?
answer: Hugely, and the reason is mobile. Most people deciding where to eat are searching on their phone, and Google found that 53% of them leave a site that takes longer than three seconds to load. That's a hungry, ready-to-book diner gone before they ever see your menu. Most restaurant sites are slow; yours loads in about 1.5s, so the people who find you actually stay, look, and book.

question: Is SEO included?
answer: Yes and no, and the difference matters. SEO splits into two halves: the work on your site, and the work off it. Everything on your site I handle to a high standard with every build: fast load times, clean code, flawless mobile, proper page structure, meta tags, and a sitemap. That foundation is the part most restaurant sites get wrong, and a slow or messy site quietly caps how well you can rank no matter what else you do. The off-site half, things like backlinks, citations, and ongoing content, is a specialist discipline of its own and isn't part of the build. If you want to take it further, I'll point you the right direction.

question: Are the three free designs really free?
answer: Completely free, with no obligation. Tell me about your venue and I'll design three real concepts around your actual business, so you can see exactly how your site could look before deciding anything. Love it, and we'll talk next steps. If it's not for you, walk away and owe nothing. Get your 3 free designs
